scope_note: Cert [493]: QA Witt Tower Ocean SST Return-Rank Persistence (NDBC Buoys). Operator: ANOMALY-LEVEL rank bins on monthly-deseasonalised daily WTMP (water temperature, NDBC buoy STDMET hourly -> daily mean); b=floor(rank(SST_anom[t])*27/N), e_val=floor(rank(SST_anom[t+1])*27/N); SST_anom=WTMP_daily-monthly_mean; a=b+2*e_val (A2 derived, raw); signal a<=6; target SST_anom[t+2]. FINDING: STRONGEST PERSISTENCE -- all 4 buoys autocorr_lev 0.916-0.964 (vs temperature [492] 0.691-0.766); pooled n_sig=2479 (4.432x expected), EXCEEDS temperature [492] 3.40x and rivers [490] 2.69x; pooled_excess=-1.510C; pers_p=0.0 all 4. DISCRIMINATION LADDER: EEG 0.72x anti-persistent < rivers 2.69x < temperature 3.40x < SST 4.43x MOST PERSISTENT. Mechanism: ocean thermal inertia (heat capacity >> atmosphere); SST changes <<1C/day vs atmospheric temperature 3-5C/day -> much stronger consecutive rank correlation. Data: NDBC STDMET hourly 2000-2024, buoys 41001(NW Atlantic, 34.8N 72.4W) / 46059(NE Pacific, 38.1N 129.9W) / 51003(N Pacific, 19.1N 160.6W) / 46066(N Pacific, 52.8N 155.0W). orbit_family on (b, e_val, m) Singularity-type pairs. Primary sources: Rayner (2003) doi:10.1029/2002JD002670; Deser (2003) doi:10.1175/1520-0442(2003)016<0057:SSTICO>2.0.CO;2. Parents: cert [490] (rivers), cert [492] (temperature). 6/6 PASS.
